techniques.name: Cross-Domain Transfer
techniques.description: Borrow a mechanism from an unrelated field and apply it to the target domain
techniques.promptTemplate: Generate {{count}} software idea(s) for the domain '{{domain}}' using cross-domain transfer. Pick a mechanism from a completely unrelated field (biology, logistics, finance, game design, etc.) and explain how that mechanism, applied to {{domain}}, produces a novel software product. {{#problemArea}}Focus on the problem area: {{problemArea}}.{{/problemArea}} Each idea must be software-only — no hardware, manufacturing, or lab work. Output each idea as a JSON object with all required fields.

techniques.name: Forced Analogy
techniques.description: Force an analogy between a random object/concept and the target domain
techniques.promptTemplate: Generate {{count}} software idea(s) for the domain '{{domain}}' using forced analogy. Pick a random object or concept (a kitchen appliance, a sports rule, a musical instrument) and force an analogy between it and {{domain}}. What software product emerges from that analogy? {{#problemArea}}Focus on the problem area: {{problemArea}}.{{/problemArea}} Each idea must be software-only — no hardware, manufacturing, or lab work. Output each idea as a JSON object with all required fields.

techniques.name: Contradiction Search
techniques.description: Find the biggest contradiction or unsolved tension in the domain and resolve it
techniques.promptTemplate: Generate {{count}} software idea(s) for the domain '{{domain}}' using contradiction search. Identify the biggest unsolved contradiction or tension in {{domain}} — two things that both seem necessary but conflict. Design a software product that resolves that contradiction. {{#problemArea}}Focus on the problem area: {{problemArea}}.{{/problemArea}} Each idea must be software-only — no hardware, manufacturing, or lab work. Output each idea as a JSON object with all required fields.

techniques.name: Morphological Matrix
techniques.description: Enumerate key dimensions and combine unusual dimension values
techniques.promptTemplate: Generate {{count}} software idea(s) for the domain '{{domain}}' using a morphological matrix. List 3-4 key dimensions of solutions in {{domain}} (e.g., delivery method, user type, data source, interaction model). Pick an unusual combination of values across dimensions. Design a software product from that combination. {{#problemArea}}Focus on the problem area: {{problemArea}}.{{/problemArea}} Each idea must be software-only — no hardware, manufacturing, or lab work. Output each idea as a JSON object with all required fields.
